extra.reasoning: The property (monotonicity in hemoptysis: setting hemoptysis=true never lowers acuity) holds. The input variable `hemoptysis` does not appear in any rule's condition or action. All five rules are gated only on the other five inputs: raise_stiff_neck (stiff_neck), raise_dyspnea (dyspnea), raise_abdominal_pain (abdominal_pain), raise_back_pain (back_pain), raise_vision_loss (vision_loss). The default acuity:=routine fills the output when no rule fires. Since hemoptysis is never read, toggling it (with all other inputs fixed) cannot change which rules match, cannot change the per-output priority resolution, and cannot change the default. Therefore acuity is invariant to hemoptysis: acuity(hemoptysis=true) equals acuity(hemoptysis=false) for every assignment of the other inputs. Equal acuity is trivially "no lower than before," so the monotonicity property is satisfied across the entire input space.
